mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate relative to trailing CPI inflation compresses the equity risk premium by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever the real policy rate remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Rank IC between real fed funds level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive real-rate periods show equal or higher average returns than negative real-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 3-month moving average, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as growth expectations reprice.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE breakout events are followed by positive or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 conditional on trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts jointly indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer and residential demand that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations for consumer-sensitive sectors are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline occur in the same month
failure_condition: Joint decline events show no statistically significant difference in 60-day forward returns versus baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in industrial production growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression, causing S&P 500 returns to weaken over the subsequent trading week as forward guidance from industrial firms turns cautious.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration periods in late-cycle regimes show 5-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than expansion-average returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds trailing year-over-year CPI inflation by more than 1 percentage point, the resulting sharply positive real policy rate signals a restrictive monetary regime that suppresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon through sustained discount-rate pressure and tightening credit conditions.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL YoY inflation > 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Periods with real rate > 1pp show 60-day forward SPX returns not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.10) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects healthy labor demand and consumer income growth, which supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month in the absence of offsetting inflation shocks.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM growth and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative PAYEMS growth months outperform positive growth months on average.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime with elevated federal funds rates, a decline in housing starts signals amplified interest-rate sensitivity of the housing sector, foreshadowing broader demand contraction and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as rate-sensitive sectors underperform.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening regimes (FEDFUNDS above 4%) fail to produce below-median 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in industrial production growth combined with a rising unemployment rate indicates a joint supply-and-labor-side contraction that amplifies recession risk beyond what either indicator predicts alone, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as risk premia spike abruptly.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline AND UNRATE MoM increase occur concurrently
failure_condition: Concurrent INDPRO decline and UNRATE increase events fail to produce negative average 5-day forward returns, or the effect is not distinguishable from single-series signals (interaction term insignificant at p > 0.10).
